method: An outlier is a post at 3x or more its creator's median of their last 10 posts, or 1M+ views on an education topic. This first batch comes from the 17 July research; metrics carry their sourcing and its limits. Sweeps append from here.

outliers.description: Hardest GCSE Maths Question Explained (only 1 percent of students got it right)
outliers.metric: 58.1K likes, 1,855 comments (Google-indexed TikTok snippet, observed 17 Jul 2026; view count unsourced)
outliers.whyItWorked: A difficulty flex plus a percentage dare pulls in strong students and the curious at once, and the comment section fills with people posting answers and arguing, which feeds distribution. It is a repeatable series: the account has run the same hook for at least two years at consistent five-figure likes.
outliers.reworkIdea: The A-level version anchored to real sources rather than invented percentages: 'the A-level Chemistry question the examiners' report says most students lost marks on', one clean sub-60-second walkthrough, examiners' report on screen as the receipt. The dare survives; the sourcing stays honest.

outliers.description: Study roulette anniversary post ('this is what kicked off my TikTok journey')
outliers.metric: 97.3K likes, 53.1K saves, 5,021 shares (read off the live video page, 17 Jul 2026; TikTok hides view counts logged out)
outliers.whyItWorked: A named, copyable system is the strongest asset type in the niche: the 55 percent save-to-like ratio means students bookmark it to rebuild it themselves, and it spawned fan tutorials and a paid Tes teacher template. The name itself becomes the searchable hook.
outliers.reworkIdea: Give Blurt and Fix the identical named-method treatment: 'watch me blurt an entire A-level topic in 10 minutes', the blurting template visibly on screen, gaps found and fixed on camera, free template PDF as the comment-keyword follow-up. Same play later for the seven diagnostic archetypes as a series.

outliers.metric: 596.8K views on a 26.3K-follower account (read off the live profile's Popular tab, 17 Jul 2026), roughly 23x his baseline
outliers.whyItWorked: Contrarian exam-technique hooks can massively outrun account size because TikTok ranks per video, not per account. His title system (X is NOT Y, FATAL mistakes, if only you knew this before) is a ready-made swipe file, and it worked from a small dormant account.
outliers.reworkIdea: Launch-week hook series in the same shapes, resolved with the high-yield replacement: 'Rereading your notes is NOT revision', 'Your mock grade does NOT predict your result', 'The 5 marks A-level Biology students give away in every essay'. Each ends on the replacement technique, with the diagnostic as the where-do-I-start step.

outliers.description: May 2025 GCSE English Literature predictions video (and the 2025 predictions TikTok)
outliers.metric: 290,000+ views on YouTube (AFP, 15 Aug 2025; re-covered by ITV, Nov 2025); companion TikTok predictions post at 54.7K likes, 1,428 comments (snippet, 17 Jul 2026)
outliers.whyItWorked: Exam-week predictions are the single highest-urgency format in the niche, treated by students as a two-way ritual (1,400+ comments). They are also the cautionary tale: AQA warned against them, students blamed misses for grades, and the backlash was national news twice. Reach bought with trust is expensive.
outliers.reworkIdea: Own the same night-before search window without predicting: 'Tomorrow is AQA Biology Paper 2. Here is what the spec allows them to ask, and the answer structure that scores whatever comes up.' Same urgency, same timing, zero prediction risk, and it compounds trust instead of spending it.

outliers.description: GCSE Maths Predicted Paper for Foundation Edexcel is up!
outliers.metric: 33.4K likes, 406 comments (Google-indexed snippet, 17 Jul 2026), roughly 10x her typical post; the Higher-tier twin framed as a reply-to-comment did 5,059 likes
outliers.whyItWorked: A named, recurring free asset dropped as an event ('is up!') becomes a search brand in its own right: TikTok auto-generates discover pages for her predictions by name. Splitting one asset into two posts via the reply-to-comment frame doubles reach for the same work and manufactures social proof.
outliers.reworkIdea: A named seasonal drop students search for by name: 'The Results Day Pack is up' in early August 2026, built from the seven results-day and UCAS blog posts that already exist, delivered via comment keyword. Then the reply-to-comment twin: 'Replying to @student: yes, here is the Clearing script version.'

outliers.description: Results-day redemption post: kicked out in Year 11, double A* and a B at A-level, addressed to 'every teacher who doubted me'
outliers.metric: 58.6K likes, 540 comments on a small personal account (observed 17 Jul 2026; posted around 14 Aug 2025)
outliers.whyItWorked: Underdog receipts plus vindication plus direct address is the most repeatable results-day format, and it fires from tiny accounts because the emotion is cohort-wide. It only works in the August window, which makes it a calendar play.
outliers.reworkIdea: Waleed's own origin story told in this exact structure in the week before 13 Aug 2026 (his grades are approved proof points; no student grade-outcome claims exist until his August results data). From results day onward, student transformation stories with written permission become the recurring version.

outliers.description: Confession post: 'I got a C on one of my final mocks before my A*'
outliers.metric: 1,639 likes (video-page snippet, 17 Jul 2026; like count only, no view count sourced)
outliers.whyItWorked: Vulnerability plus authority is the trust format: the C-to-A* arc is exactly the story anxious A-level students want to believe about themselves, and it lands with no product pitch attached. She pairs it with mark-scheme pattern-spotting, which is near-identical to Waleed's diagnose-before-you-treat frame.
outliers.reworkIdea: His own confession in his own voice: the topic he nearly flopped, the moment the system replaced the panic, told plainly to camera. It doubles as the setup for the diagnostic: 'I did not need more hours, I needed a diagnosis. That is what I built for you.'

outliers.description: The harsh reality of being a doctor (why I left medicine)
outliers.metric: 41.9K likes, 447 comments vs a 500 to 3K like baseline on his study clips (Google-indexed snippets, 17 Jul 2026)
outliers.whyItWorked: On short form the doctor-identity story outperforms the technique content by 10x to 40x even on an account built for learning science. The personal stakes are the hook; the study method is why people stay. His cutdown-style TikToks underperform, so native-to-platform beats repurposed.
outliers.reworkIdea: 'Why I finish an NHS shift and then teach A-level students in the evening' as a native, direct-to-camera reel: identity content as the trust layer on a schedule, technique content as the daily utility layer. Never just clip long-form; shoot for the format.

outliers.creatorName: Unnamed (case study by Social Growth Engineers, 31 Mar 2026; creator not attributable from the accessible article)
outliers.description: Faked 27-hours-straight study timelapse ('Studying for 27hrs straight for my finals')
outliers.metric: 11.9M views per the case study (single source, observed 17 Jul 2026)
outliers.whyItWorked: An extreme number in the first frame plus the relatable finals-grind visual is one of StudyTok's most reliable viral shapes, and in this case the obvious fakery became comment bait that the algorithm read as engagement. The number is the hook; the honesty is optional for them, mandatory for Waleed.
outliers.reworkIdea: The honest version keeps the numeric hook and adds receipts: 'I went through 6 mark schemes in one sitting. The same 3 phrases keep costing students marks.' Real, bounded, verifiable numbers from his own marking and teaching, with the payoff list delivered inside 45 seconds.

outliers.description: Confession post: 'Just because I got an A* and now spend my life teaching & tutoring biology, people assume I found it easy. I didn't - it was brutal! ... I actually got a C on one of my final mocks which massively knocked my confidence.' She credits learning the spec, spotting mark-scheme patterns, and mentoring a Year 12.
outliers.metric: 1,639 likes (from TikTok video-page search snippet, observed 17 Jul 2026; no view count exposed)
outliers.whyItWorked: Vulnerability plus authority. The C-to-A* arc is exactly the emotional story A-level students want to believe about themselves, and it lands under a pure #alevelbiology tag with no product pitch.

outliers.description: "What might be on paper 3 for AQA Biology" exam-prediction clip (her highest-engagement format: predicting what will come up on a specific 2026 exam paper)
outliers.metric: ~11.6K likes, 179 comments (search-snippet metric, observed 17 Jul 2026; view count not sourced)
outliers.whyItWorked: Exam predictions tied to a specific named paper and board create urgency and mass-save behaviour in exam season. This is her single strongest hook type and recurs every cycle (2024, 2026 versions all exist).

outliers.description: Study roulette anniversary post: 'Can't believe that was a year ago, this is what kicked off my TikTok journey' (4:41, #studyroulette #spreadsheet). Marks the method video that launched the account in ~July 2024.
outliers.metric: 97.3K likes, 734 comments, 53.1K saves, 5,021 shares (observed on the video page 17 Jul 2026; TikTok hides view counts from logged-out video pages, so likes/saves are the sourced metrics)
outliers.whyItWorked: A named, copyable SYSTEM. The save count is the tell: 53.1K saves against 97.3K likes (a roughly 55% save-to-like ratio) means students bookmark it to rebuild the spreadsheet themselves. It spawned an ecosystem: fan tutorials (e.g. tiktok.com/@teeadeyale.6/video/7530261983329832214), a 36-post 'Study roulette' playlist on her own profile, and a paid Tes teacher template crediting her (updated 28 Oct 2025).

outliers.description: Physics short: vectors vs scalars ('There are only six vector quantities...'). Tight, exam-syllabus fact delivered fast.
outliers.metric: 92,200 plays, 776 likes, 32 shares, posted 11 Mar 2023 (video-detail JSON, observed 17 Jul 2026)
outliers.whyItWorked: Roughly 50 to 90x her recent per-video baseline (~1 to 2K plays). A single searchable exam fact ('only six vector quantities') that students hunt for at revision time; no fluff, pure recall.

outliers.description: His four top-performing Meta reels tracked by Motion's creator intelligence page, ahead of his 2026 brand-deal run (Adobe, Grammarly, SoFi, Microsoft Surface, YouTube, Google Chrome, Campus.edu, Google Gemini)
outliers.metric: 19.7M, 13M, 11M and 10.7M views on the four highlighted reels (Motion creator page, dated 2026, observed 17 July 2026; no individual post dates given)
outliers.whyItWorked: Same engine as always: one concrete tip, scripted second-by-second, prop or scenario hook in the first moment. The Motion page confirms this format is now also his sponsorship vehicle: 57 organic posts vs 15 Meta-sponsored ads, with brands paying an estimated 10k to 50k dollars per partnership to borrow the format.

outliers.description: "The harsh reality of being a doctor" (his story of leaving medicine), Oct 2022 upload that remains his standout TikTok
outliers.metric: 41.9K likes, 447 comments (Google-indexed TikTok page, observed 17 Jul 2026); a March 2023 re-post of the same theme got 11.3K likes and 173 comments
outliers.whyItWorked: It is 10x to 40x his normal TikTok engagement (his study-tip cutdowns indexed by Google show 500 to 3K likes). The doctor-who-quit identity story outperforms the technique content on short-form; the personal stakes are the hook, the study advice is what people stay for. Directly transferable to Waleed's NHS-doctor founder story.
